routesPage.title: Routing
routesPage.description: Rules that decide which destinations each event goes to. Evaluated by priority — the first match wins, up to a final rule.
routesPage.unreachableDescription: One or more routes are shadowed by an earlier 'final' route and will never be evaluated. Adjust priority/condition.
routesPage.noCatchAllDescription: No route with an empty condition (match-all) marked as 'final' was found. Unmatched events will fall back to Wazuh.
routesPage.emptyDescription: Without routes, everything goes to Wazuh (back-compat). Create rules to route by severity, vendor, tenant…
routesPage.flowBannerText: The flow topology (sources → routing → destinations, with live throughput) now lives on a dedicated page.
routesPage.deleteDialogDescription: Delete "{{name}}"? The audit history is preserved.
routesPage.bulkEnableDialogDescription: Selected routes will start evaluating events.
routesPage.bulkDisableDialogDescription: Selected routes will stop evaluating events until re-enabled.
routesPage.reorderRouteAria: Reorder route {{name}} — press space and use arrow keys
routesPage.systemRouteDescription: Protected system route. New destinations get an auto-created broadcast route pointing to them.

activityModal.noActivityPrefix: No recent activity on this route (routing dormant until

dryRun.title: Simulate routing
dryRun.description: Evaluates saved routes WITHOUT delivering anything. Test a synthetic event or use recent events (tenant audit buffer).
dryRun.noSamplesEvaluated: No samples evaluated (no recent events in the audit buffer, or no test event).

destinationsPage.title: Destinations
destinationsPage.description: First-class outputs — where collected events are delivered, besides Wazuh.
destinationsPage.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, ID or type…
destinationsPage.emptyDescription: Create a destination (Splunk HEC, Syslog, JSONL…) to start routing events.
destinationsPage.noResultsDescription: No destination matches the selected filters.
destinationsPage.deleteDialogDescription: Delete "{{name}}"? This action is permanent. DLQ entries already recorded are preserved for forensics.

health.title: Destination health
health.noRecentTraffic: No recent traffic on this destination.

dlq.title: Dead-letter (rejected events)
dlq.empty: No events in this destination's DLQ.
dlq.reprocessAllDescriptionWithCount: Reprocess all {{count}} events in this destination's DLQ? They will be re-queued for delivery. The DLQ is not cleared on a new failure.
dlq.reprocessAllDescriptionEmpty: Reprocess events from this destination's DLQ.
dlq.reprocessEntryDescription: Reprocess only event {{eventId}}? It will be re-queued for delivery.

tap.title: Live data-tap (recent events, redacted)
tap.empty: No recent events for this destination (or dormant traffic).

piiRuleEditor.jsonInvalidSwitch: Invalid JSON — fix it before switching to structured mode.
piiRuleEditor.keepPrefixAria: Number of characters to keep in the prefix
piiRuleEditor.keepSuffixAria: Number of characters to keep in the suffix
piiRuleEditor.octetsAria: Number of IP octets to keep
piiRuleEditor.structuredHintPrefix: Add rules to mask/remove PII fields before delivery. Use paths in the format

auditPanel.title: Audit trail
auditPanel.empty: No audit entries found for this route.
auditPanel.rollbackAria: Roll back route to snapshot from {{date}}
auditPanel.rollbackSuccess: Route "{{name}}" rolled back to snapshot from {{date}}.
auditPanel.rollbackDialogDescription: Roll back "{{name}}" to the snapshot from {{date}} ({{action}})? The current route will be replaced.

conditionEditor.noConditions: No conditions — this rule matches every event.

routeForm.destinationRequired: Select at least one destination (or use action=drop).
routeForm.piiPathRequired: Every PII rule needs a field path defined.
routeForm.wazuhDefaultDescription: Default route — receives every event with full delivery.
routeForm.destinationsEmptyLabel: No destinations. Create one under Destinations.
routeForm.finalCheckboxLabel: Final (stops on 1st match; off = clones and continues → fan-out)
routeForm.piiDescriptionPrefix: Masks/pseudonymizes/removes fields BEFORE delivering to this route — the same source arrives intact in the lake and masked in the SIEM. Fields only under
routeForm.reductionFlagsNoticeTitle: What actually turns these levers on
routeForm.reductionFlagsNoticeBody: The backend flags REDUCTION_SAMPLE_ENABLED, REDUCTION_SUPPRESS_ENABLED and REDUCTION_TRIM_ENABLED are ON by default (ADR-0015). What actually activates reduction is this route's own configuration: sample_percent starts at 100 (keeps everything) and suppress_allow starts at 0 (off) — while they stay that way, no event is dropped. Aggregation (REDUCTION_AGGREGATE_ENABLED), by contrast, remains off by default.
routeForm.protectDetectionDescription: On by default as a fail-safe: this route is never sampled or aggregated, even when the reduction levers are active in the environment.
routeForm.unprotectedWarningBody: This route CAN now be sampled/suppressed by the reduction levers. Events relevant to detection may never reach the SIEM. Only use this on routes where reducing volume is safe (e.g. low-value telemetry, not security events).
routeForm.unprotectDialogDescription: Routes with protect_detection off can have events sampled or suppressed by the reduction levers — including events that would feed detection. This is a conscious risk decision, not automatically reversible. Confirm only if you are certain this route carries no signal relevant to detection.
routeForm.samplePercentHelper: 100 = no sampling (byte-identical); <100 = only that fraction reaches destinations.
routeForm.samplePercentHelperProtected: Disabled — a protected route is never sampled.
routeForm.suppressKeyHelper: CSV of routing labels forming the signature (vendor, platform, event_type, severity_id, stream, integration_id, customer_id, organization_id, data_geography). Only these exist — a log field such as src_ip is rejected. Empty = no suppression.
routeForm.dropRawLabel: Drop the raw event on this delivery
routeForm.dropRawHelper: Removes the raw block from the payload delivered by this route. The normalized (OCSF) event is preserved. Use it on the SIEM, which bills by volume, and leave it off on the data lake route, where the raw event is needed for forensics. Usually the single largest saving.
routeForm.suppressAllowHelper: How many events with the same signature pass per window. 0 = suppression off.

topology.title: Flow topology
topology.throughputDescription: Edge thickness is proportional to events per minute; destination color indicates health status (green = healthy, yellow = degraded, red = unavailable).
topology.legendAriaLabel: Topology legend: throughput and destination health status encoding
